How to watch Jensen Huang’s Nvidia GTC 2026 keynote
articles.description: GTC — which stands for GPU Technology Conference — is Nvidia's flagship annual event, where the chipmaker typically uses the spotlight to announce new products, champion partnerships, and lay out its vision for the future of computing. Huang's keynote will focus on Nvidia's role in the future of com

articles.title: Sales automation startup Rox AI hits $1.2B valuation, sources say
articles.description: Rox, founded in 2024 by the former chief growth officer of New Relic, offers an AI-native alternative to CRM tools.

articles.title: Channel Surfer lets you watch YouTube like it’s old-school cable TV
articles.description: The web app lets you turn on YouTube and surf channels like you're watching cable TV in the '90s.

articles.title: Why Rivian is holding the $45,000 base model R2 until ‘late 2027’
articles.description: Rivian spent the last two years promoting the R2 as a $45,000 SUV. But buyers won't be able to pay that price until late next year -- if at all.

articles.title: Substack launches a built-in recording studio
articles.description: Substack creators can pre-record video conversations with up to two guests, then publish directly on the platform.

articles.title: Facebook Marketplace now lets Meta AI respond to buyers’ messages
articles.description: When buyers inquire about an item’s availability, sellers can use Meta AI to automatically draft replies using information from their listing, such as the description, availability, pickup location, and price.

articles.title: Tinder tries to lure people back to online dating with IRL events, virtual speed dating
articles.description: Tinder just got a major revamp as it attempts to reengage its user base and attract younger daters. This includes in-person events, AI enhancements, and even virtual speed dating.

articles.title: Atlassian follows Block’s footsteps and cuts staff in the name of AI
articles.description: Atlassian laid off 10% of its workforce, around 1,600 people, as the company looks to funnel more funds to AI.

articles.title: Lucid Motors shows off robotaxi concept called ‘Lunar’
articles.description: The concept was a highlight of Lucid's investor day, in which the company detailed its ambitions to push deeper into autonomy while pumping out new, affordable EVs.

articles.title: Humanoid robotics maker Sunday reaches $1.15B valuation to build household robots
articles.description: Sunday is on a quest to build a household humanoid robot called Memo that helps with tasks like laundry and clearing the table. The company emerged from stealth late last year and already has 1,000 people on its waitlist.

articles.title: Webflow buys AI content-generation platform Vidoso to bolster its marketing suite
articles.description: Founded in 2024, Vidoso uses large language models to help organizations generate marketing collateral like images, presentations, video clips, blog posts, and social media content.

articles.title: Bumble introduces an AI dating assistant, ‘Bee’
articles.description: Bumble's new AI assistant Bee will move the dating app beyond the swipe by matching people based on compatibility and goals.

articles.title: Law enforcement shuts down botnet made of tens of thousands of hacked routers
articles.description: An international law enforcement operation shut down a service called SocksEscort, which allegedly helped cybercriminals all over the world launch ransomware and DDoS attacks, as well as distribute child sexual abuse material.

articles.title: A writer is suing Grammarly for turning her and other authors into ‘AI editors’ without consent
articles.description: Journalist Julia Angwin is leading a class action lawsuit against Grammarly for violating her privacy and publicity rights.

articles.title: Tesla becomes a utility in the UK, setting up showdown with Octopus Energy
articles.description: The new Tesla Energy Ventures division was approved by Ofgem to sell electricity directly to customers.

articles.title: Gumloop lands $50M from Benchmark to turn every employee into an AI agent builder
articles.description: As companies race to adopt AI, Benchmark general partner Everett Randle believes the key to success lies in empowering every worker with AI superpowers, and Gumloop’s intuitive agent builder is an example of the kind of tool that will unlock that potential.

articles.title: Alexa+ gets a new ‘adults only’ personality option that curses but won’t do NSFW content
articles.description: The new Sassy style can curse and roast you, but the fun ends there.

articles.title: Wonderful raises $150M Series B at $2B valuation
articles.description: The funding round, led by Insight Partners, comes just four months after Wonderful raised a $100 million Series A.

digest:
## 🔬 Major Technical Developments

### Executing programs inside transformers with exponentially faster inference
URL: https://www.percepta.ai/blog/can-llms-be-computers
Percepta claims exponential inference speedups by executing programs within transformer architecture.
If validated, this represents a fundamental shift in how we think about LLM compute costs. The approach suggests treating transformers as programmable substrates rather than pure pattern matchers—watch for benchmarks and whether this translates to production workloads.

### Document poisoning in RAG systems: How attackers corrupt AI's sources
URL: https://aminrj.com/posts/rag-document-poisoning/
Enterprise RAG deployments face a new attack surface as adversaries learn to corrupt retrieval sources.
As companies rush to deploy retrieval-augmented generation for internal knowledge bases, security teams are playing catch-up. This matters for every enterprise AI deployment—expect security audits and vendor due diligence to now include RAG poisoning vectors.

### Bringing Chrome to ARM64 Linux Devices
URL: https://blog.chromium.org/2026/03/bringing-chrome-to-arm64-linux-devices.html
Google officially expands Chrome support to ARM64 Linux, signaling broader ARM ecosystem commitment.
This follows the ARM momentum in servers (AWS Graviton, Ampere) and edge devices. For infrastructure investors, it validates the thesis that ARM workloads are moving beyond mobile into general computing.


## 💼 Business & Market Signals

### US private credit defaults hit record 9.2% in 2025, Fitch says
URL: https://www.marketscreener.com/news/us-private-credit-defaults-hit-record-9-2-in-2025-fitch-says-ce7e5fd8df8fff2d
Private credit default rates hit 9.2%—highest on record—creating distressed opportunities.
This is the headline for your morning call. The private credit boom of 2021-2023 is now showing cracks. Special situations funds should be modeling which sectors and vintages are most exposed; sponsor-backed software and healthcare services historically correlate with these cycles.

### Atlassian follows Block's footsteps and cuts staff in the name of AI
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/12/atlassian-follows-blocks-footsteps-and-cuts-staff-in-the-name-of-ai/
Atlassian cuts 1,600 employees (10% of workforce) to reallocate capital toward AI initiatives.
This confirms the pattern: mature enterprise software companies are using AI as both cost-cutting justification and reinvestment thesis. Watch for margin expansion in Q2 earnings and whether AI-attributed productivity gains materialize or remain narrative.

### Wonderful raises $150M Series B at $2B valuation
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/12/wonderful-raises-150m-series-b-at-2b-valuation/
Wonderful hits $2B valuation just four months after $100M Series A—Insight Partners leads.
Velocity of capital deployment here is notable. Four months between rounds at these sizes suggests either exceptional traction metrics or frothy late-stage conditions returning. Worth tracking as a sentiment indicator for growth equity.

### Humanoid robotics maker Sunday reaches $1.15B valuation to build household robots
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/12/humanoid-robotics-maker-sunday-reaches-1-15b-valuation-to-build-household-robots/
Sunday hits unicorn status with 1,000 waitlist customers and a household robot called Memo.
Humanoid robotics valuations remain disconnected from commercial reality. 1,000 waitlist sign-ups supporting a billion-dollar valuation is pure optionality pricing. Compare to Tesla Bot and Figure AI—this space is crowded with capital chasing long-dated payoffs.


## 📋 Case Studies & Deep Dives

### ATMs didn't kill bank teller jobs, but the iPhone did
URL: https://davidoks.blog/p/why-the-atm-didnt-kill-bank-teller
Historical analysis reveals mobile banking—not ATMs—drove branch consolidation and teller job losses.
Classic case of misattributed technological disruption. The lesson for AI impact modeling: the obvious automation threat often isn't the actual displacement vector. Second-order effects and behavior changes matter more than direct substitution.

### Malus – Clean Room as a Service
URL: https://malus.sh
Satirical "clean room" service highlights growing anxiety around AI training data provenance and legal exposure.
The humor lands because the pain is real. Every foundation model company faces copyright litigation risk; enterprises deploying AI need to understand their liability chain. This is why IP indemnification clauses are now standard in enterprise AI contracts.

### The 8 Levels of Agentic Engineering
URL: https://www.bassimeledath.com/blog/levels-of-agentic-engineering
Framework for categorizing AI agent sophistication from basic automation to autonomous systems.
Useful mental model for evaluating agentic AI startups and enterprise deployments. Most production systems today sit at levels 2-3; the gap to levels 6+ represents both opportunity and execution risk.


## ⚡ Signal vs. Noise

The Atlassian layoffs and Gumloop's $50M raise tell the same story from opposite ends: enterprises are consolidating headcount while betting that low-code agent builders can distribute AI capability to remaining staff. Meanwhile, the private credit default spike at 9.2% suggests the easy money era's hangover is arriving faster than expected—distressed tech debt will be a theme for the next 18 months. The humanoid robotics valuations (Sunday at $1.15B) remain venture theater; the real infrastructure play is in the picks-and-shovels like ARM expansion and transformer efficiency research.
